100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 17, 1926, tensions escalated in the Middle East as British forces clashed with Arab nationalists in Palestine, heightening fears of widespread unrest. In the United States, the ongoing debate over Prohibition intensified, with calls for reform gaining traction amidst rising crime and corruption. Meanwhile, in Germany, the government faced mounting pressure as economic instability continued to challenge the Weimar Republic.
